2.q: Overexpression of PagGRF15 and PagHAK6 in transgenic lines improved salt tolerance by enhancing Na+ transport and modulating H2O2 accumulation in poplar.

6.q: in poplar, it improved growth, FV/FM, non-photochemical quenching values and resistance to H2O2-induced oxidative stress under salt stress... revealing that PagGRXC9 enhances salt tolerance by reducing photoinhibition.

15.q: PagHB7a and PagHB7b are homologs in Populus with 98% sequence similarity and belong to the HD-ZIP I subfamily.

27.q: Growth-regulating factor 15 (PagGRF15) and its target, high-affinity K+ transporter 6 (PagHAK6), were identified as an important regulatory module in the salt stress response.

28.q: PagHB7a was directly regulated by the ABA-responsive element (ABRE) binding proteins (PagAREB1s)... PagAREB1s were identified as upstream transcriptional activators of PagHB7a under salt stress.

29.q: The full-length CDS of PagAREB1-2, PagAREB1-3, PagAREB1-4, and PagABF3 were cloned... combinations co-transformed into yeast... for chromogenic detection of interactions; PagAREB1s identified as upstream transcriptional activators of PagHB7a.

30.q: PagAREB1s were identified as upstream transcriptional activators of PagHB7a under salt stress, tested by Y1H and dual-luciferase with PagAREB1-4 binding the ABRE in the PagHB7a promoter.

31.q: The promoter region of PagHB7 containing ABRE cis-elements was inserted into the LUC reporter; PagABF3 effector tested with the PagHB7a promoter in dual-luciferase assay.

32.q: the ABA‐responsive element (ABRE) binding proteins (PagAREB1s) directly bind to PagAFP2a promoter and activate its expression.

33.q: The PagAFP2a, PagNCED3, PagNCED5, PagRD26, and PagNAC002 promoter sequences containing the ABRE cis-element were amplified... co-transformed with AD-PagAREB1-2/PagAREB1-3/PagAREB1-4/PagABF3 construct.

34.q: The PagAFP2a, PagNCED3, PagNCED5, PagRD26, and PagNAC002 promoter sequences containing the ABRE cis-element were used in Y1H and dual-luciferase assays with PagAREB1s.

35.q: All the results indicate that PagbHLH35 can improve salt tolerance by enhancing ROS scavenging in transgenic poplars.

36.q: PagHB7a was significantly induced by salt and abscisic acid (ABA) treatments; PagHB7a, a salt stress- and ABA-inducible transcription factor, acts as a positive regulator of salt tolerance.
